questions.question: 'Keystoning' (converging verticals) in architecture photos is caused by…
options: Tilting the camera up or down so parallel lines appear to lean
questions.reviewHint: Tilting the camera makes vertical lines converge - the keystoning effect.

questions.question: One way to reduce converging verticals is to…
options: Keep the camera level, or correct perspective in editing
questions.reviewHint: Keeping the camera level (or using perspective correction) keeps verticals straight.

questions.question: A specialised lens designed to control perspective in architecture is a…
questions.reviewHint: Tilt-shift lenses let you correct converging verticals in-camera.

questions.question: Leading lines are valuable in architecture because buildings…
options: Are full of strong lines that can guide the eye
questions.reviewHint: Architecture is rich in lines - use them to lead the eye through the frame.

questions.question: Symmetry and patterns are common in architecture photography because…
options: Buildings often contain repeating, symmetrical structures
questions.reviewHint: Repeating windows, columns and facades make symmetry and pattern natural subjects.

questions.question: 'Blue hour' is popular for architecture because…
options: Ambient sky light balances nicely with building/interior lights
It is the brightest time of day
It increases dynamic range of the sensor
questions.reviewHint: At blue hour the sky's brightness balances artificial lighting for even exposures.

questions.question: HDR techniques help architecture shots that…
options: Have a very wide range between bright windows and dark interiors
questions.reviewHint: HDR blends exposures to hold detail in both bright windows and dark interiors.

questions.question: A wide-angle lens is often chosen for architecture to…
options: Fit large structures or interiors into the frame
questions.reviewHint: Wide lenses capture large buildings and tight interiors that won't otherwise fit.

questions.question: A tripod and remote release help architecture work by…
options: Enabling sharp images in low light and precise composition
questions.reviewHint: They allow sharp low-light shots and careful, repeatable framing.

questions.question: When photographing building details, a useful approach is to…
options: Isolate textures, patterns or sections rather than always shooting the whole structure
questions.reviewHint: Detail shots of texture, pattern and form complement full-building images.
